text.title: api-gateway-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-gateway-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Rate-limiting plugin configured with `policy: local` (in-memory) in a multi-replica gateway deployment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each replica tracks its own counter; effective limit = N × configured limit. Always use `policy: redis` for shared state.

failure-mode.description: Gateway re-implementing fine-grained authorization (resource ownership, RBAC at row level).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The gateway lacks domain knowledge of the resource. Logic duplication drifts from the service and creates security gaps. Keep fine-grained auth in the service.

failure-mode.description: UI-driven config edits that bypass GitOps.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Next decK sync silently reverts the change. Production behavior diverges from git head, making incident debugging impossible.
